title: Web Data, Fetch, Storage, and Security Boundaries · Spiral Review
summary: Load and cache public service data while handling hostile input, offline states, privacy, and response failures. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a resilient privacy-aware service-data client after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use fetch with explicit method, headers, body, status checks, parsing, abort, and failure-state behavior.
Construct and parse URL, URLSearchParams, FormData, and encoded payloads without manual delimiter bugs.
Parse, validate, transform, and serialize JSON while accounting for unsupported values and hostile object shape.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Fetch Response · changed case
steps.instruction: review case — Fetch unknown status JSON containing a hostile "" label, cancel request 4, render with textContent, minimize stored data, and expose a recoverable error without leaking credentials. Which action demonstrates Fetch Response without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use fetch with explicit method, headers, body, status checks, parsing, abort, and failure-state behavior.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

stimulus.title: Origin Permissions case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Fetch unknown status JSON containing a hostile "" label, cancel request 1, render with textContent, minimize stored data, and expose a recoverable error without leaking credentials.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: CORS is an authentication system that protects server data from all clients.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain origin, CORS, CSP, credentials, workers, and permission prompts as layered browser boundaries. Artifact under review: a resilient privacy-aware service-data client.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Origin Permissions, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Origin Permissions: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Fetch unknown status JSON containing a hostile "" label, cancel request 2, render with textContent, minimize stored data, and expose a recoverable error without leaking credentials.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Explain origin, CORS, CSP, credentials, workers, and permission prompts as layered browser boundaries.
